2603.16370.title: FederatedFactory: Generative One-Shot Learning for Extremely Non-IID Distributed Scenarios
2603.16370.paper_summary: Federated Learning (FL) enables distributed optimization without compromising data sovereignty. Yet, where local label distributions are mutually exclusive, standard weight aggregation fails due to conflicting optimization trajectories. Often, FL methods rely on pretrained foundation models, introducing unrealistic assumptions. We introduce FederatedFactory, a zero-dependency framework that inverts the unit of federation from discriminative parameters to generative priors. By exchanging generative modules in a single communication round, our architecture supports ex nihilo synthesis of universally class balanced datasets, eliminating gradient conflict and external prior bias entirely. Evaluations across diverse medical imagery benchmarks, including MedMNIST and ISIC2019, demonstrate that our approach recovers centralized upper-bound performance. Under pathological heterogeneity, it lifts baseline accuracy from a collapsed 11.36% to 90.57% on CIFAR-10 and restores ISIC2019 AUROC to 90.57%. Additionally, this framework facilitates exact modular unlearning through the deterministic deletion of specific generative modules.
2603.16370.paper_summary_zh: 聯邦學習（FL）使得分散式優化得以實現，而不會妨礙數據主權。<lb/>然而，當本地標籤分佈互相排斥時，標準的權重聚合因為優化軌跡的衝突而失效。<lb/>通常，FL 方法依賴於預訓練的基礎模型，這引入了不切實際的假設。<lb/>我們提出了 FederatedFactory，一個零依賴的框架，將聯邦的單位從判別參數轉換為生成先驗。<lb/>通過在一次通信回合中交換生成模塊，我們的架構支持從無到有合成普遍類別平衡的數據集，徹底消除了梯度衝突和外部先驗偏見。<lb/>在包括 MedMNIST 和 ISIC2019 在內的多樣醫學影像基準測試中的評估顯示，我們的方法恢復了集中式的上限性能。<lb/>在病理異質性下，它將基線準確率從崩潰的 11.36% 提升至 90.57%，並將 ISIC2019 的 AUROC 恢復至 90.57%。<lb/>此外，這個框架通過對特定生成模塊的確定性刪除，促進了精確的模塊化遺忘。
2603.16370.author: Andrea Moleri et.al.
2603.16370.authors: Andrea Moleri, Christian Internò, Ali Raza, Markus Olhofer, David Klindt, Fabio Stella, Barbara Hammer
